steps.title: Identify what the sentence introduces
steps.content: The opening “A painting by 19th-century artist Katsushika Hokusai,” introduces a painting as the topic. When a sentence introduces a noun this way followed by a comma, the clause that follows should have that noun as its subject to maintain proper sentence structure.

steps.title: Find the choice where the painting is the subject
steps.content: Choice D makes “The Great Wave off Kanagawa” (the painting) the subject of the main clause—the painting employs visual allegory to convey its meaning. This correctly maintains the painting as the focus introduced at the start. Choices A and B make “meaning” the subject, and C makes “visual allegory” the subject; neither properly continues from the introduction. That matches choice D — D is correct.
